topic.what: SISA-based machine unlearning for RL-based ransomware detection using DQN and DDQN
topic.how: Shards training data into 5 partitions; trains isolated RL agents per shard; evaluates one-shard fast-unlearning by retraining only affected shard after 5% sample deletion
topic.why_matters: Enables GDPR right-to-be-forgotten compliance for ransomware detectors without full retraining; bridges privacy-aware ML with security-critical RL systems

characteristics.what_it_measures: Ransomware detection accuracy of RL agents
characteristics.evidence: 5-fold stratified cross-validation on 2000 Windows 11 behavioral samples
characteristics.context: DDQN marginally outperforms DQN with tighter variance

characteristics.evidence: Q-score = Q(s,a=1) - Q(s,a=0) computed from learned action-value function
characteristics.context: Near-perfect ranking; enables confidence-based ROC analysis for RL agents

characteristics.what_it_measures: F1 degradation after one-shard SISA unlearning
characteristics.evidence: 5% samples deleted from single shard; only affected shard retrained
characteristics.context: Negligible utility loss confirms practical unlearning for privacy compliance

characteristics.what_it_measures: Computational cost of one-shard unlearning vs full SISA
characteristics.evidence: CPU-only Google Colab Pro; 5 shards with independent RL agents
characteristics.context: One-shard unlearning reduces cost to near-baseline levels; full SISA is ~5x baseline
DDQN shows improved stability; DQN has larger variance across folds
Cost-sensitive reward (+1 correct, -2 FN, -0.5 FP) balances asymmetric ransomware risk

good_for: production ransomware detectors requiring GDPR compliance
privacy-aware security systems needing post-deployment data deletion
CPU-constrained enterprise environments without GPU resources
cost-sensitive cybersecurity applications with asymmetric risk profiles
requires: Windows 11 behavioral telemetry with API/filesystem features
structured behavioral data amenable to Q-value estimation
labeled ransomware/benign dataset with family attribution

novelty: First systematic evaluation of SISA-based unlearning for RL-based ransomware detection
Controlled comparison of DQN vs DDQN under identical privacy-driven deletion settings
Demonstration of negligible utility degradation (≤0.05% F1 drop) with one-shard fast-unlearning
Q-score-based ROC evaluation enabling confidence ranking for RL agents under unlearning
open_problems: Multi-shard and sequential deletion scenarios not evaluated
No formal verification of forgetting (membership inference, oracle-based tests)
Actor-critic and policy-gradient methods excluded from evaluation
Large-scale unlearning with adversarial conditions not addressed
